case_name: Ibuprofen reduction from surface water for small drinking water system
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible treatment process chains for ibuprofen-contaminated surface water intended for drinking water production in a small, low-energy system.

input_data.scenario_background: A small town draws raw water from a river-influenced reservoir and has identified ibuprofen as the primary trace organic contaminant of concern. The utility needs a practical drinking water treatment upgrade that is proven, compact enough for a small site, and compatible with low-energy operation. The system prefers technologies with straightforward operation and does not want to rely on high-pressure membranes.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should be suitable for drinking water production with strong reduction of ibuprofen and compliance with conventional microbial and physical water quality requirements.
secondary_goals: use established technology suitable for a small utility
avoid unnecessary concentrate or brine management

engineering_constraints.residuals_management: limited on-site residuals handling capacity; periodic spent media replacement is acceptable but large sludge or brine streams are undesirable
engineering_constraints.robustness_requirement: stable performance under seasonal source-water variability is required

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation: reduce suspended solids and part of natural organic matter to protect downstream adsorption
core_unit_functions.media_filtration: remove clarified particles and provide conventional surface water polishing
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_adsorption: adsorb ibuprofen and other trace organic compounds from finished water precursor stream
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbial control for drinking water safety

applicability_rationale: ibuprofen is a trace organic contaminant for which activated carbon is a well-established control approach in drinking water treatment
pretreatment with coagulation and filtration is appropriate for surface water with moderate turbidity and DOC
GAC is compatible with small-system operation and avoids the energy demand of high-pressure membranes
the overall train is conventional, proven, and aligns well with low-energy drinking water production
potential_risks: high DOC can shorten GAC bed life and increase replacement or reactivation frequency
if upstream solids removal is inconsistent, carbon fouling and premature breakthrough may occur
ibuprofen breakthrough requires monitoring because trace organic removal is not directly visible from routine plant indicators

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation: destabilize particles and reduce background organic matter
core_unit_functions.filtration: polish remaining solids for conventional treatment compliance
core_unit_functions.powdered_activated_carbon_dosing: provide adsorptive removal of ibuprofen with dose adjustment during contamination events
core_unit_functions.disinfection: ensure microbiological safety of treated water

applicability_rationale: PAC is a recognized option for episodic or moderate trace organic contaminant control in surface water treatment
dose flexibility can be useful if ibuprofen concentrations vary seasonally or after wastewater influence events
the train remains lower energy than membrane alternatives and uses familiar conventional units
potential_risks: PAC performance depends strongly on dose, contact time, and competition from natural organic matter
increased solids and carbon handling can be challenging for a small utility
performance may be less stable than fixed-bed GAC for continuous long-term removal targets

core_unit_functions.coagulation_flocculation: remove particles and reduce background organic load
core_unit_functions.media_filtration: protect oxidation and adsorption-biological polishing stages
core_unit_functions.ozonation: oxidize ibuprofen and transform micropollutants into more biodegradable products
core_unit_functions.biological_activated_carbon: adsorb residual organics and biologically polish ozonation byproducts

applicability_rationale: ozone followed by BAC is an established advanced treatment concept for many pharmaceuticals and other trace organics
the combination can provide broader micropollutant control than adsorption alone
it is technically strong for surface water applications with moderate organic matter when advanced treatment is justified
potential_risks: ozonation increases electrical demand and operational complexity compared with adsorption-only options
bromate formation risk must be assessed if bromide is present in source water
small systems may find ozone generation and process control burdensome

core_unit_functions.bank_filtration_or_riverbank_infiltratio: provide natural attenuation, particulate removal, and some biodegradable organic reduction before treatment
core_unit_functions.aeration: restore dissolved oxygen and improve water quality prior to adsorption
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_adsorption: polish remaining ibuprofen and trace organics

applicability_rationale: where feasible, bank filtration is a low-energy pretreatment approach often used with surface-water-influenced supplies
combining natural pretreatment with GAC can lower engineered treatment burden
this option aligns with low-energy goals if suitable hydrogeology and land access are available
potential_risks: site-specific hydrogeology may make implementation infeasible or slow to permit
ibuprofen removal by bank filtration alone is not reliable enough, so GAC polishing remains important
seasonal source-water variability and travel-time uncertainty require careful validation

core_unit_functions.ultrafiltration_or_reverse_osmosis: provide physical separation, with reverse osmosis offering strong dissolved contaminant rejection
core_unit_functions.activated_carbon_polishing: remove residual organics and improve taste and odor
core_unit_functions.stabilization: adjust finished water chemistry after intensive treatment

applicability_rationale: high-pressure membrane treatment can reduce many dissolved contaminants including pharmaceuticals
it may be considered if the utility later faces a broader suite of difficult contaminants beyond ibuprofen
potential_risks: reverse osmosis creates concentrate that is difficult for a small inland system to manage
energy use and operator attention are inconsistent with the stated low-energy small-system constraints
ultrafiltration alone is not a reliable primary barrier for dissolved ibuprofen without additional advanced treatment

preferred_rank_pattern: activated_carbon_based treatment with conventional surface water pretreatment should rank first
PAC should rank as a practical alternative but generally below GAC for stable continuous removal in a small drinking water plant
high-energy membrane options should not rank first under low-energy small-system constraints

must_reflect: low-energy preference should favor adsorption and conventional pretreatment over ozone or reverse osmosis
small-system practicality should favor robust and comparatively simple unit processes
options with large sludge, brine, or highly specialized control burdens should rank lower

must_contain: why activated carbon is appropriate for ibuprofen as a trace organic pharmaceutical
why pretreatment is needed for surface water with moderate turbidity and DOC
why ozone-based and membrane-based options rank lower under the stated constraints

should_mention: competition from natural organic matter reducing activated carbon capacity
breakthrough monitoring needs for trace organic removal
bromate or oxidation byproduct concerns for ozonation
concentrate management and energy burden for reverse osmosis
